purpose: Single source of truth for visual consistency. Paste the relevant blocks into every Google Flow / Veo prompt so every clip looks like the same show.

art_style.base: soft 3D Pixar-style render, rounded shapes, gentle rim lighting, no harsh shadows, cinematic shallow depth of field, child-friendly and cozy
art_style.lighting: warm soft key light, soft ambient fill, no hard contrast
art_style.negative: no on-screen text, no watermark, no logos, no realistic violence or gore, no scary or distorted faces, no fast strobing or flashing, no photorealistic humans

color_palette.note: Keep these four colours dominant in every shot for instant brand recognition.

Mimi.full_descriptor: Mimi is a small, toddler-proportioned fox cub with a big round head and a little body. Her fur is warm orange (#FF8A65) with a soft cream chest and cream inner ears. She has large round expressive teal eyes (#4FC3F7), tiny rounded ears, a small black nose, and a fluffy orange tail with a cream tip. She wears a small bright-yellow scarf (#FFD54F). Soft 3D Pixar-style render, rounded shapes, gentle rim lighting. She looks curious, kind, and gently brave.
Mimi.proportions: oversized head, small rounded body, short limbs, toddler-like
Mimi.personality: curious, kind, gently brave, never frightened or aggressive

voice.en: bright young female, warm, slightly breathy

flow_tips: Paste Mimi's FULL descriptor (characters.Mimi.full_descriptor) verbatim into EVERY single clip prompt - never abbreviate her to just 'the fox'. Consistency comes from repetition.
Reference the art_style.base string word-for-word in every prompt so the render style never drifts.
Lock ONE seed value for the entire job and reuse it on every clip. Pick it once (e.g. 12345) and never change it mid-job. Same seed + same descriptor = visually matching clips.
After your first clip comes out good, download it and reuse its first frame (or the clip itself) as the reference / start image for the next clips so Flow has a visual anchor.
Always paste the negative list (art_style.negative) to suppress text, watermarks, and scary content.
Introduce at most ONE new background element per scene; keep the palette colours dominant.
Visuals are language-agnostic - render each scene only ONCE. The same clip is reused for EN, FR, and ES; only the narration and burned-in subtitles change.
Keep each generated clip to a single scene/shot. assemble.py stitches the numbered clips together in order.
Flow Fast credits are limited (~50 clips/month on AI Pro), so plan your scene count first: a 4-scene short + an 8-scene long = 12 clips per job.
